reference: I have exercise-induced asthma, which has never been a huge problem for me until now. I have no health insurance and I am sick with a pretty bad cough. Every once in a while, I cough so badly that I have an attack and I can't breathe or stop coughing enough to use my inhaler. To add to that, the inhaler I am using is over five years past the expiration date. So, what I am wondering is, is there any way to fix these problems without health insurance?
text: I have exercise-induced asthma, which has never been a huge problem for me until now. I have no health insurance, and I am sick with a pretty bad cough. Every once in a while, I cough so badly that I have an attack, and I can't breathe or stop coughing enough to use my inhaler. To add to that, the inhaler I am using is over five years past the expiration date. So, what I am wondering is, is there any way to fix these problems without health insurance?
